top_signals.title: Investment Committee's Message: Don't Overfocus on SpaceX IPO
top_signals.summary:
Matt Kennedy, senior strategist at Renaissance Capital, and Josef Schuster, founder and CEO of IPOX Schuster, join Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Deals." SpaceX is offering 555.6 million shares at a fixed price of $135 each, which would raise about $75 billion. The stock is set to start trading on June 12. The IPO is expected to rank as the biggest ever, topping Saudi Aramco’s $29.4 billion listing in 2019.

 (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: AI will help young workers 'mature' faster by automating grunt work, Thoma Bravo says, amid youth job crisis
top_signals.summary: "If you look at our associates, they're spending a lot less time doing models or comparables than before," Orlando Bravo, founder of Thoma Bravo, told CNBC.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Trump suggests he may not renew trade deal with Mexico and Canada
top_signals.summary: ‘We don’t need anything that they have,’ US president says of 2020 trilateral agreement

top_signals.title: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, April 2026
top_signals.summary: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit decreased in April 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit decreased from $56.6 billion in March (revised) to $55.9 billion in April, as exports increased more than imports. The goods deficit decreased $2.4 billion in April to $83.7 billion. The services surplus decreased $1.7 billion in April to $27.8 billion. Full Text

top_signals.title: Two Matsuya companies team up in Tokyo on premium beef dishes
top_signals.summary: The Matsuya Premium Ginza store offers upscale takeout dishes including premium beef-on-rice bowls.
